title: heliophila cedarbergensis
intro: Heliophila cedarbergensis, also known as Cedarberg Heliophila or Yellow Buttons, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This annual plant is native to the Western Cape Province in South Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Heliophila cedarbergensis include its ability to reduce fever, soothe wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into a tea or infused oil, which can be applied topically to the skin or consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
